content.full_text:
Phillip M. Miller
424 West Commonwealth, Fullerton, California 92632 • (714) 990-2918

DEC 20 3 55 PM '90

December 17, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallibrin
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 'J' Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Mr. Hallibrin:

I am Assemblyman Ross Johnson's district Administrative Assistant. In addi-
tion, outside normal working hours, I am a professional speaker and a stand-
up comic.

I'm concerned about my outside activities being affected by the new ethics
regulations.

My activities are in three areas:

Professional Speaker: I speak to corporations, associations and man-
agement clubs on management and interpersonal communication skills.
I'm paid from $500 to $2,000 per speech. I never speak on political
subjects or anything dealing in any way with my work for Ross. I
never tell a client that I work for the legislature.

Stand-up comic: I work as a comic in comedy clubs. None of my em-
ployers know that I work for the legislature. I'm paid from $50 to $200
per appearance.

Community Services classes at community colleges: I teach classes at
several Southern California community colleges on interpersonal
communications. In obtaining the jobs I never indicate that I work for
the legislature; some colleges have since discovered my position with
Ross. I average about $200 per class.

Please tell me if these activities conflict with the new regulations.

Thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

Phillip M. Miller

sections.question: Please tell me if these activities conflict with the new regulations.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor, an Assemblyman's district administrative assistant, seeks guidance on whether his outside professional activities as a speaker, stand-up comic, and community college instructor conflict with new ethics regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed descriptions of three outside activities and a direct question seeking ethics guidance. No formal conclusion section is present as this is correspondence from a member of the public to FPPC counsel. Document ID 90770 appears to be the request identifier assigned by FPPC.

embedding.summary: Phillio M. Miller, district administrative assistant to Assemblyman Ross Johnson, inquires whether his outside professional activities (professional speaking, stand-up comedy, and community college teaching) violate new ethics regulations.
